items.title: Watch: Why PM Modi’s Indonesia visit matters for India | Explained
items.description: Nearly 40% of India’s maritime trade passes through one of the world’s busiest shipping lanes, the Strait of Malacca. And the country that sits at the gateway to that strategic chokepoint is Indonesia. That’s why Prime Minister Narendra Modi’s visit to Indonesia, his first bilateral visit to the country since 2018, is about much more than diplomacy. It is about securing India’s maritime interests, expanding economic partnerships and strengthening its position in an increasingly competitive Indo-Pacific. So, why does Indonesia matter so much to India?

items.title: LG Energy Solution forecasts 77% operating profit fall in Q2 on weak EV demand
South Africa deploys troops as anti-migrant protests escalate
items.description: South Africa has deployed more than 3,000 soldiers across the country to reinforce security as anti-migrant protests intensify, with organisers vowing to stage demonstrations every week.

items.title: Why Ukrainian strikes on annexed Crimea are a blow to Putin
items.description: Eastern Europe correspondent Sarah Rainsford explains why Crimea is a target for Kyiv, and why the peninsula matters to Vladimir Putin.

items.title: Syria's 'security challenges' main obstacle to attracting investors
items.description: Explosions rocked Damascus on July 7 as France’s president is meeting with his Syrian counterpart in a surprise, landmark visit. Emmanuel Macorn was not at the hotel at the time of the explosions and had already arrived in the presidential palace. The two leaders were about to kickoff a series of meetings where contracts regarding Syria's reconstructions were going to be signed when the explosions happened. FRANCE 24's Dana Alboz tells us more from Damascus, and how Syrian authorities are hoping to overcome security challenges to attract investors.

items.title: Anyone who doesn’t feel for Palestinians is not human: Egypt coach Hassan
items.description: Hossam Hassan uses his FIFA World Cup news conference to raise awareness about the plight of Palestinians in Gaza.

items.title: Around 2% of Gaza’s child population killed by Israeli security forces : UN report
items.description: The latest report by the UN independent commission of inquiry captures Israel’s crimes against Palestinian children

items.title: How Volkswagen's huge workforce became a liability
items.description: The struggling German carmaker plans to cut up to 100,000 jobs from its 630,000-strong workforce. How did VW's headcount get so bloated, and will the cuts be enough to survive the Chinese EV onslaught?

items.title: Morocco beats Canada to book a place in the World Cup quarter-finals
items.description: Following their 3-0 victory, the Atlas Lions will now face tournament favourites France, in Boston next Thursday.

items.title: Thousands flee raging wildfires in southern Europe
items.description: The fire has nearly tripled in size since early Sunday (July 5, 2026), devouring 4,600 hectares and leaving a firefighter and a resident injured

items.title: Cape Verde World Cup team hopes tournament success will boost next generation
items.description: Cape Verde’s World Cup journey has come to an end, but the island nation leaves the tournament with valuable memories and a new level of recognition.

items.title: Bandit raid leaves nine farmers dead in northwest Nigeria
items.description: At least nine farmers have been killed and several others abducted in a deadly raid on a farming community in Nigeria's Kaduna State, underscoring the growing insecurity threatening rural livelihoods as armed gangs intensify attacks during the country's planting season.

items.title: Confirmed Ebola cases in DR Congo rise to 1,502
items.description: The Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of the Congo continues to escalate, with confirmed cases climbing to more than 1,500 and at least 473 people dead, according to government figures.

items.title: US toddler found in pool and declared dead later found alive
items.description: Five hours after the young boy was pronounced dead next to a suburban swimming pool in the US state of Arizona, police were notified that the child was breathing again at a local hospital.

Austrian court convicts 2 former Syrian officials of abuse
items.description: A Vienna court has convicted two former Syrian security officials, including the former intelligence chief in Raqqa, for offenses including torture and sexual assault against opponents of Bashar Assad's regime.

items.title: Iran begins farewell to Supreme Leader Khamenei
items.description: Before dawn, hundreds of mourners gathered in Tehran as Iran began days of funeral ceremonies for Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, who was killed months earlier in a joint US-Israeli airstrike.

Senegal's power struggle: Referendum to decide on constitutional reform

items.description: Sick leave in Germany has hit a new record, with workers taking an average of nearly 20 days off per year. As Chancellor Merz cracks down, how does the country's absenteeism compare to other countries?

items.title: Israeli parliament backs first step towards October 7 inquiry
items.description: Israel's parliament approved the first reading of a bill on Monday to establish a commission of inquiry into the security failings surrounding Hamas's October 7, 2023 attack, though opposition lawmakers boycotted the vote, arguing the proposed body would lack independence.

items.title: Sri Lanka prison riots leave 26 dead and more than 100 injured
items.description: The two days of violence at Negombo Prison are the worst prison riots in the country in years.

items.description: Experts warn that cuts to US research funding is weakening the country’s scientific and technological capacity.

items.title: Kielce pogrom in Poland: 80th anniversary of 1946 massacre
items.description: Just over a year after World War II ended, a mob in the Polish city of Kielce killed around 40 Jews, some of them Holocaust survivors. A lie about ritual child murder escalated the violence.
